sourceMovie.title: A Time to Kill
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto A Time to Kill (1996) as a crime theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Talented Mr. Ripley
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If A Time to Kill clicked for you, The Talented Mr. Ripley (1999) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after A Time to Kill?
faq.answer: Try The Talented Mr. Ripley, Primal Fear, Cape Fear—each shares DNA with A Time to Kill while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is A Time to Kill?
faq.answer: A Time to Kill (1996) is best described as Crime, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is A Time to Kill worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
